assertions.reason: The real user surface still does not expose a runs-table id or any way to drive a run to terminal state and then resend completion against that same run.
The real user surface still does not expose a cancellable runs-table id that can be used for late-callback testing.

Invalid tokens are rejected and signed callbacks with a study-table id get past auth but fail v.id("runs") validation, so a valid heartbeat could not be exercised without a real runs-table id from the user surface.
The callback receiver accepts signed requests, but the user surface still does not expose a real runs-table id that can be used for milestone insertion.
The callback receiver accepts signed requests, but the user surface still does not expose a real runs-table id that can be used for completion finalization.
The callback receiver accepts signed requests, but the user surface still does not expose a real runs-table id that can be used for failure finalization.
The worker and callback receiver are reachable, but the real user surface still does not expose the full execute-run payload and real runs-table id needed for an end-to-end success path.

frictions: The real callback receiver now accepts signed requests, but using a study-table id immediately fails v.id("runs") validation.
The runs tab exposes rich persona text but still no run identifier in visible text or button attributes.
blockers: No real runs-table id is exposed on the user surface, so valid heartbeat/milestone/completion/failure or duplicate/late-callback scenarios cannot be exercised end-to-end.
The real user surface still does not expose the full execute-run payload needed for a success-path worker request.
